text.title: monorepo-turborepo Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for monorepo-turborepo as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Circular package dependencies: packages/a depends on packages/b, which depends on packages/a.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Turborepo cannot compute a valid execution order for a cycle; the build hangs or fails with an opaque error. Extract the shared code into a third package with no internal dependencies.

failure-mode.description: Phantom dependencies: package uses a library that works in development because it is hoisted to the root node_modules, but the package.json does not declare it as a dependency.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The build works locally but fails on a clean install or after publishing because the transitive dep is no longer guaranteed to be present. Always declare every import as an explicit dependency.

failure-mode.description: Hardcoded relative imports across packages: import { Button } from '../../../packages/ui/src/button'.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Relative cross-package imports bypass the exports map, break on publish, and are invisible to Turborepo's dependency graph. Always use the package name: import { Button } from '@org/ui'.

failure-mode.description: Outputs path mismatch: turbo.json declares outputs: ["dist/**"] but the build tool emits to out/.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Turborepo caches an empty directory. Future CI runs get a cache hit with no files, then fail during the next task that consumes the outputs. Use --summarize to inspect what was actually cached.

failure-mode.description: Undeclared environment variables in env / globalEnv that affect the build output.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The same task hash is computed for both NODE_ENV=development and NODE_ENV=production builds, so the prod artifact gets served as the dev build (or vice versa). Every env var that changes output must be declared.

failure-mode.description: Caching dev and start tasks, or caching non-deterministic tasks.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Dev servers are non-deterministic and never complete. Caching them stores incomplete state and breaks subsequent runs that depend on the server being available.

failure-mode.description: Caching tasks as dev when they should not be cached because they have side effects or random output.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Any task with "cache": false must also have "persistent": true to prevent Turborepo from treating the task as one-shot.
